title: Sweet Silky Russian Beef Dumplings
description: A quick-fix Russian dish featuring minced beef, served in a sweet and silky sauce. This dairy-free-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the minced beef, chopped onion, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Gradually add the flour to the beef mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for about 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a small egg. You should end up with around 20-25 dumplings.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the dumplings. Cook for 10-12 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
While the dumplings are cooking, heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the apple cider vinegar and honey to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the sauce by half, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a thick and silky consistency.
Remove the cooked dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and add them to the skillet with the sweet sauce.
Toss the dumplings in the sauce to coat and serve hot.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
